---
# SETTLED — 2026-06-06. The central content type of the guide.
title: "Mechanism Name"
aliases: []          # Other names designers use for this mechanism
family: ""           # Family 1–6 (Structure, Puzzles, Story, Social, Space, Systems)
media: []            # Filter tags: interactive-fiction, live-game, tabletop, puzzle-hunt
facets:
  effects: []        # What feeling or outcome does this create?
  problems: []       # What design roadblock does this solve?
  components: []     # What physical/mechanical parts are required?
  genre: []          # Genre lens for routing: mystery, horror, fantasy, sci-fi, historical, children-ya, etc. (cross-cut, like media)
provenance: ""       # corpus | researched | synthesis
---

*Silhouette: A single-line "jizz" definition of the mechanism's impression.*

<!-- THE READABLE HEAD (THE SPARK) -->
<!-- 1-3 short paragraphs of evocative "Naturalist" prose. 
     No headers, no bullet points. Focus on the feeling and purpose. -->

## The Mechanism (How it works)

<!-- THE SCANNABLE BODY (THE BLUEPRINT) -->
<!-- Use Diagnostic Headers (e.g. ## The Trigger) to group reflective prose.
     No bullets in main prose flow. Move data-dense lists to Tables or Insets.
     CROSS-REFERENCES GO HERE, INLINE: wherever another mechanism becomes of import
     to the reader at that point, link it in the prose ("...the order they arrive,
     which is [Clue Sequencing & Chains](clue-sequencing-and-chains.qmd)..."). Handle
     disambiguation in context, not as a list. Link the actual sibling .qmd file. -->

<!-- CALLOUTS — sparing, to highlight the exceptional. AT MOST TWO per article, and only when a
     point genuinely warrants being pulled out of the prose (not something that belongs in the
     regular flow). There is NO fixed "Design Judgement" section and no fixed order or position:
     a callout sits wherever its point lands in the article. Aim for ONE Specimen per article,
     placed naturally. The Field Note and Hazard are OPTIONAL flavours, used only when the advice
     or failure-mode is a genuine standout; otherwise weave that judgement into the prose.
     Available flavours (place where warranted, do not stack a rote set):
       ::: {.callout-note}   ### The Specimen   A real-world example. [corpus]
       ::: {.callout-tip}    ### The Field Note  Standout mentor advice. [synthesis]
       ::: {.callout-warning} ### The Hazard     A standout failure mode. [synthesis]
-->

::: {.callout-note}
### The Specimen
A real-world example of this mechanism in the wild, placed wherever it fits the article. [corpus]
:::

## Where this fits

<!-- A short, practical close (two paragraphs, one section). Prose, not lists.
     Cross-references are links, as in the body. This is orientation, NOT a recap. -->

**The bigger picture.**
<!-- How this mechanism sits in the wider design landscape / its family: what it
     depends on, what depends on it, the role it plays. -->

**Next steps.**
<!-- What a designer practically reaches for next, with inline links to those articles. -->

## Sources
<!-- Real EXTERNAL citations (books, essays, the works themselves) for every factual
     [corpus]/[researched] claim. The vault is never cited. Harvest the feeding corpus
     note's Research Basis; research the gaps. -->
